title: Finite Walsh-Hadamard Transform Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Walsh-Hadamard bad transform-coefficient QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact transform replay computes the second Hadamard coefficient as -2 while a malformed row claims -1, isolating a compact equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional orthogonal-transform rows only when they add distinct exact-linear solver pressure; keep fast Walsh-Hadamard algorithms, Fourier theory, and numerical stability in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed order-4 Hadamard matrix, source vector, transform vector, inverse reconstruction, and energy identity as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false transform-coefficient row through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting coefficient.
Expose the pack through the matrix computation learner and consumer query surfaces.
Keep general Walsh-Hadamard transform theorems, fast algorithms, Fourier-analysis analogues, and numerical stability claims marked Lean/numerical horizon.
